title: A finite row maps the zero input to zero

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Applying any finite row to the zero input vector gives zero.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
a is a finite real-valued row
the input vector is identically zero
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite row application is zero.

declarations.name: finiteRowApply
declarations.statement: def finiteRowApply {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, a i * x i

declarations.name: finiteRowApply_zero
declarations.statement: theorem finiteRowApply_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a : ι → Real) : finiteRowApply s a (fun _ => 0) = 0

tasks.input: Translate the fact that a finite row maps the zero vector to zero into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteRowApply_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a : ι → Real) : finiteRowApply s a (fun _ => 0) = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding finiteRowApply and simplifying the zero products.
